protocol.name: wlr_foreign_toplevel_management_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1

description.summary: list and control opened apps

interface.description:
The purpose of this protocol is to enable the creation of taskbars and docks by providing them with a list of opened applications and letting them request certain actions on them, like maximizing, etc.
After a client binds the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1, each opened toplevel window will be sent via the toplevel event

event.name: toplevel

description.summary: a toplevel has been created

event.description:
This event is emitted whenever a new toplevel window is created. It is emitted for all toplevels, regardless of the app that has created them.
All initial details of the toplevel(title, app_id, states, etc.) will be sent immediately after this event with the corresponding events in zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1.

request.name: stop

description.summary: stop sending events

request.description:
Indicates the client no longer wishes to receive events for new toplevels. However the compositor may emit further toplevel_created events, until the finished event is emitted.
The client must not send any more requests after this one.

event.name: finished

description.summary: the compositor has finished with the toplevel manager

event.description: This event indicates that the compositor is done sending events to the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_manager_v1. The server will destroy the object immediately after sending this request, so it will become invalid and the client should free any resources associated with it.

interface.name: zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1

description.summary: an opened toplevel

interface.description:
A zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object represents an opened toplevel window. Each app may have multiple opened toplevels.
Each toplevel has a list of outputs it is visible on, conveyed to the client with the output_enter and output_leave events.

event.name: title

description.summary: title change

event.description: This event is emitted whenever the title of the toplevel changes.

event.name: app_id

description.summary: app-id change

event.description: This event is emitted whenever the app-id of the toplevel changes.

event.name: output_enter

description.summary: toplevel entered an output

event.description: This event is emitted whenever the toplevel becomes visible on the given output. A toplevel may be visible on multiple outputs.

event.name: output_leave

description.summary: toplevel left an output

event.description: This event is emitted whenever the toplevel stops being visible on the given output. It is guaranteed that an output_enter event for this output was emitted before this event.

request.name: maximize

description.summary: request that the toplevel be maximized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be maximized. If the maximized state actually changes, this will be indicated by the state event.

request.name: unset_maximize

description.summary: request that the toplevel be unmaximized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unmaximized. If the maximized state actually changes, this will be indicated by the state event.

request.name: minimize

description.summary: request that the toplevel be minimized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be minimized. If the minimized state actually changes, this will be indicated by the state event.

request.name: unset_minimize

description.summary: request that the toplevel be unminimized

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unminimized. If the minimized state actually changes, this will be indicated by the state event.

request.name: activate

description.summary: request that the toplevel be activated

request.description: Request that this toplevel be activated on the given seat. There is no guarantee the toplevel will be actually activated.

request.name: close

description.summary: request that the toplevel be closed

request.description: Send a request to the toplevel to close itself. The compositor may currently not be able to close the toplevel at the time the request is received. In that case, the request will be ignored.

request.name: set_rectangle

description.summary: the rectangle which represents the toplevel

request.description: The rectangle of the surface that is representing the toplevel. This rectangle is in the "surface local coordinates" of the layer surface interface. It allows the compositor to define a placement for the toplevel (e.g. for the minimize animation).

event.name: state

description.summary: the toplevel state changed

event.description: This event is emitted immediately after the zlw_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 is created and each time the toplevel state changes, either because of a compositor action or because of a request in this protocol.

event.name: done

description.summary: all information about the toplevel has been sent

event.description:
This event is sent after all changes in the toplevel state have been sent.
This allows changes to the toplevel properties to be seen as atomic, even if they happen via multiple events.

event.name: closed

description.summary: this toplevel has been destroyed

event.description: This event means the toplevel has been destroyed. It is guaranteed there won't be any more events for this zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1. The toplevel itself becomes inert so any requests will be ignored. After the client receives this event, it should destroy this object.

enum.name: state

description.summary: types of states on the toplevel

enum.description: The different states that a toplevel can have. These have the same meaning as the states with the same names defined in xdg-toplevel

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object

request.description:
Destroys the zwlr_foreign_toplevel_handle_v1 object.
This request should be called either when the client does not want to use the toplevel object any more or after the closed event to avoid leaking the object.

event.name: parent

description.summary: parent change

event.description:
This event is emitted whenever the parent of the toplevel changes.
No event is emitted when the parent of this toplevel is null.

request.name: set_fullscreen

description.summary: request that the toplevel be fullscreened

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be fullscreened on the given output. If the fullscreen state actually changes, this will be indicated by the state event.

request.name: unset_fullscreen

description.summary: request that the toplevel be unfullscreened

request.description: Requests that the toplevel be unfullscreened. If the fullscreen state actually changes, this will be indicated by the state event.
